trade.summary: Singapore is one of Australia's largest trading partners, functioning as a major hub for Australian trade throughout Southeast Asia. Australia exports LNG, gold, services, and education; Singapore exports refined petroleum, electronics, financial services, and chemicals. The Singapore-Australia Free Trade Agreement (SAFTA), updated under CSFTA, has deepened bilateral commerce.

diplomatic.summary: Australia-Singapore relations are elevated to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership — one of Australia's most significant bilateral relationships. Both nations share Commonwealth ties, similar legal systems, and aligned views on regional security, trade, and multilateralism. Annual leaders summits occur. Singapore serves as Australia's key partner in Southeast Asia.

military.summary: Australia hosts Singapore military training through the Shoalwater Bay Training Area and other facilities under a longstanding Defence Cooperation Agreement. Both are members of the Five Power Defence Arrangements (FPDA). The defence relationship is among Australia's most operationally deep in Southeast Asia, with regular exercises and embedded personnel.

financial.summary: Singapore is a major financial hub handling Australian capital flows throughout Asia. GIC and Temasek (Singapore sovereign wealth funds) hold substantial Australian assets. Many Australian multinationals use Singapore as their Asia-Pacific headquarters. The two financial centres are deeply integrated.

energy.summary: Australia is a significant LNG supplier to Singapore. Singapore's role as a regional energy hub means much Australian LNG is traded through Singapore markets. An Australia-Singapore electricity link (via submarine cable) has been proposed and is under development review.

scientific.summary: Extensive scientific and educational collaboration. Australian universities have significant Singapore partnerships. Joint research in biomedical science, AI, water technology, and urban planning. Many Singaporean students undertake advanced degrees in Australia. Strong technology startup and innovation ecosystem links.
